description: Renders an explicit function y = f(x) on a 2D graph. Plots y=f(x) over the visible x-range with optional color and stroke styling. For other plot types use the dedicated expressions: paraplot (parametric), implicitplot (implicit), polarplot (polar), piecewiseplot (piecewise), inequalityplot (inequality).
utility: Plot an explicit function y = f(x) on a 2D graph

arguments.name: equation
arguments.description: Explicit function expression as a quoted ASCII math string, a function reference, or a string variable. The independent variable is auto-detected (x, t, n, etc.) — any variable not already defined in context is treated as the plot parameter.

arguments.name: range
arguments.description: Optional domain restriction for the x range

alternates.description: Plot y = f(x) over the full visible x-range
Plot with a custom stroke color
Plot a previously defined function reference
Plot with t as the independent variable — auto-detected

alternates.syntax:
f(x) = piecewise("1", "x >= -0.5 and x <= 0.5", "0")
w = 3
plot(G, "f(x)*cos(w*x)")
alternates.description: Plot a product of a piecewise function and a cosine. User-defined functions and variables (f, w) are available in the equation.

examples.scenario: Plot x squared on a plotting graph
examples.expression:
graph_1 = g2d(at(3,9), 17.5, 27.5, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
plot_1 = plot(graph_1, "x^2")

examples.scenario: Plot using a function definition reference
examples.expression:
graph_1 = g2d(at(3,9), 17.5, 27.5, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
f(x) = x^3 - 3*x + 1
plot_2 = plot(graph_1, f)

examples.scenario: Plot 1/x with explicit range on a plotting graph
examples.expression:
graph_1 = g2d(at(3,7), 21.5, 32.4, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
plot_1 = plot(graph_1, "1/(x)", range(-5, 5))

examples.scenario: Plot from function with evaluated point
examples.expression:
graph_2 = g2d(at(2,26), 20, 20, type(plotting))
f(x) = 1/x^2
plot_2 = plot(graph_2, f)
k = point(graph_2, 2, f(2))

examples.scenario: Plot a function reference h defined inline and evaluate at a point
examples.expression:
G1 = g2d(at(4,32), 12, 18, range(-1, 5, 1), range(-1, 15, 2), type(plotting))
h(x) = sin(x) + 2cos(x)
plot(G1, h)
point(G1, 1, h(1))

G1 = g2d(at(5,12), 20.3, 30.6, type(plotting))
plot_1 = plot(G1, "floor(x)", c(blue))

examples.scenario: Plot using t as the independent variable
examples.expression:
graph_1 = g2d(at(3, 9), 17.5, 27.5, range(-5, 5), range(-1.5, 1.5), type(plotting))
plot_1 = plot(graph_1, "sin(t)", c(teal))

examples.scenario: Plot product of piecewise function and cosine (windowed signal)
examples.expression:
w = 3
f(x) = piecewise("1", "x >= -0.5 and x <= 0.5", "0")
graph_1 = g2d(at(4, 4), 19, 50, range(-25, 25), range(-0.5, 1.2), type(plotting))
plot_1 = plot(graph_1, "f(x)*cos(w*x)", c(teal))
